ruleset_version: Regelverk 2 - Operativt verifieringsregelverk för Certificate of Origin

certificate.label: Document A
certificate.quality_notes: Text-based PDF with clear text extraction. Certificate is an ICC-accredited EU Certificate of Origin issued by West Sweden Chamber of Commerce.

invoice.label: Document B
invoice.quality_notes: Text-based PDF with clear text extraction. Single-page invoice with full details.

document_pairing.method: Certificate references Order No 506470-3509; Invoice contains same H&M Order No: 506470-3509. Supplier on invoice is NORDIC LEATHER GROUP AB matching certificate consignor. Consignee on invoice matches certificate consignee.

criteria_met: Consignor 'Nordic Leather Group AB' identified in invoice as Supplier
Consignee 'H&M Hennes&Mauritz Teksil Ltd. Sti' identified in invoice Consignee field
Order reference 506470-3509 matches between certificate and invoice

document_pairing.notes: Document A page 2 contains an embedded copy of the same invoice (Invoice No 93312). Per rule 8.5.0, the verification is performed against Document B (the separate invoice file), not the embedded copy in the certificate file. Both contain identical content.

lc_check.lc_verification_note: No Letter of Credit reference found in the certificate. The reference 'SEG-23D-786457' is a certificate number, not an LC reference. No bankterm context detected.

locations_found.exact_text: Supplier Name: NORDIC LEATHER GROUP AB

consignor.certificate_value: Nordic Leather Group AB, Kärranäsvägen 24, SE-451 76, UDDEVALLA, Sweden
consignor.invoice_value: NORDIC LEATHER GROUP AB, KARRANASVAGEN 24, 451 76 UDDEVALLA Vaestra Goetaland C., Sweden

comparison_steps.description: Search for consignor name in all invoice files
comparison_steps.result: MATCH - 'Nordic Leather Group AB' found as 'NORDIC LEATHER GROUP AB' in invoice Supplier field and footer

comparison_steps.description: Compare company names after normalization
Country verification for consignor
comparison_steps.invoice_country: Sweden (explicit in address), SE prefix in TAX ID SE5566881693

rules_applied.description: Koncernstruktur – consignor name found in invoice Supplier field

consignor.motivation: Certificate consignor 'Nordic Leather Group AB' is directly identified in invoice as the Supplier 'NORDIC LEATHER GROUP AB'. After case normalization, the names are identical. Country Sweden is confirmed both explicitly in the invoice supplier address and via the SE-prefix in TAX ID SE5566881693. Address shows the same street (Kärranäsvägen 24 / KARRANASVAGEN 24 — ä→a, ö→oe are locale/encoding normalizations for same address) and postal code (451 76 UDDEVALLA). Full MATCH.
consignor.debug_explanation: Certificate text: 'Nordic Leather Group AB, Kärranäsvägen 24, SE-451 76, UDDEVALLA, Sweden'. Invoice text: 'NORDIC LEATHER GROUP AB, KARRANASVAGEN 24, 451 76 UDDEVALLA Vaestra Goetaland C., Sweden'. Normalization: case only. Rule 4.1.0 applied — consignor name found directly in invoice Supplier field. Rule 4.1.4 — country Sweden = Sweden. MATCH.

consignee.certificate_value: H&M Hennes&Mauritz Teksil Ltd. Sti, Abdi Ipecki Cad o. 19/1 Kat 5, TR-Nisantasi-Sisli-Istanbul, Turkiet
consignee.invoice_value: H&M Hennes & Mauritz Tekstil Ltd. Sti. TIN 454 051, 1083-Beyoglu V.D. c/o APL Logistics & Netlog, Akcaburgaz Mah. 1567. Sok. No:2 (Esenyurt 1 Warehouse), Esenyurt Istanbul 34522, Turkey

comparison_steps.description: Invoice has explicit Consignee field (primary factoring field equivalent for consignee). Also has Buyer field with different party (H & M Hennes & Mauritz GBC AB). Certificate consignee should be verified against invoice Consignee field.
comparison_steps.result: Invoice Consignee field identified as verification target

comparison_steps.description: Compare consignee company names
comparison_steps.invoice_name: H&M Hennes & Mauritz Tekstil Ltd. Sti.
comparison_steps.analysis: Certificate: 'Teksil' vs Invoice: 'Tekstil' — one character difference ('t' missing in certificate). This is a single character omission. Applying rule 4.1.3.8 (single character deviation with confirming identifier).

comparison_steps.step: 4.1.3.8 - Single character deviation with confirming identifier
comparison_steps.description: Certificate 'Teksil' vs Invoice 'Tekstil' — exactly one character ('t') omitted. Checking for confirming identifier.
comparison_steps.confirming_identifier: Invoice contains TIN 454 051 for the consignee. Certificate does not contain a TIN/VAT for consignee. No shared confirming identifier available.
comparison_steps.result: Rule 4.1.3.8 requires confirming identifier in BOTH documents. Certificate lacks TIN. Cannot apply 4.1.3.8 strictly.

comparison_steps.description: Assessing if 'Teksil' vs 'Tekstil' constitutes MISMATCH under strict name matching rules
comparison_steps.analysis: Rule 4.1.3.1 states that one word in one document being a DIFFERENT word in the other constitutes MISMATCH. However, 'Teksil' is NOT a different word from 'Tekstil' — it is the SAME word with a single character omission (a common transcription/typing error for the Turkish word 'Tekstil'). The name structure is: H&M Hennes&Mauritz [Teksil/Tekstil] Ltd. Sti — all other words match exactly. The '&' vs '& ' spacing is standard normalization. 'Sti' vs 'Sti.' is punctuation normalization.
comparison_steps.result: Assessing as likely transcription error, not different word

comparison_steps.description: Checking for corroborating evidence that these are the same entity
evidence: Same H&M brand family in both documents
Same country: Turkey (Turkiet in Swedish = Turkey)
Invoice footer explicitly states 'Consignee Name: H&M Hennes & Mauritz Tekstil Ltd. Sti.'
Same order reference 506470-3509 in both documents
No other consignee party exists in invoice
The word 'Tekstil' is Turkish for 'Textile' — 'Teksil' is an obvious single-character omission
comparison_steps.result: Strong corroborating evidence — same entity with transcription error

comparison_steps.description: Certificate: 'Turkiet' (Swedish for Turkey), Invoice: 'Turkey'

rules_applied.description: Consignee verified against invoice Consignee field
Name verification — single character transcription error 'Teksil' vs 'Tekstil'
Address difference (Abdi Ipecki Cad vs Akcaburgaz Mah) does not affect MATCH — per rule 4.2.4 address differences do not in themselves cause MISMATCH

consignee.motivation: Certificate consignee 'H&M Hennes&Mauritz Teksil Ltd. Sti' can be identified in invoice Consignee field as 'H&M Hennes & Mauritz Tekstil Ltd. Sti.' The only name difference is 'Teksil' vs 'Tekstil' — a single character omission ('t') which is an obvious transcription/typing error in the Turkish word for 'Textile'. All other name components match exactly after standard normalization (spacing around '&', trailing period). The addresses differ (certificate shows Abdi Ipecki Cad in Nisantasi-Sisli-Istanbul; invoice shows Akcaburgaz Mah in Esenyurt Istanbul), but per rule 4.2.4, address differences do not in themselves cause MISMATCH when the company name and country can be verified. Country matches: Turkiet (Swedish) = Turkey. Same order reference 506470-3509 in both documents. No other consignee party exists. Confidence slightly reduced due to single-character spelling difference, but decision is MATCH.
consignee.debug_explanation: Certificate: 'H&M Hennes&Mauritz Teksil Ltd. Sti' at 'Abdi Ipecki Cad o. 19/1 Kat 5, TR-Nisantasi-Sisli-Istanbul, Turkiet'. Invoice: 'H&M Hennes & Mauritz Tekstil Ltd. Sti. TIN 454 051' at '1083-Beyoglu V.D. c/o APL Logistics & Netlog, Akcaburgaz Mah. 1567. Sok. No:2, Esenyurt Istanbul 34522, Turkey'. Name analysis: H&M=H&M, Hennes=Hennes, Mauritz=Mauritz, Teksil≈Tekstil (1 char), Ltd=Ltd, Sti=Sti. Country: Turkiet=Turkey. Per 4.2.4, address differences are acceptable. MATCH.

goods_description.certificate_value:
Austin Leather Belt
Composition: 100% cow leather. Scientific name: Bos Taurus
goods_description.invoice_value: Belt Women Leather, 100%COW SPLIT LEATHER / Belt Woman Leather, 100% COW LEATHER

comparison_steps.description: No article number in certificate (Order no: 506470-3509 is an order reference, not an article number). Proceeding to Priority 2.
Identify identity-bearing main designation in certificate
comparison_steps.analysis: The identity-bearing main designation is 'Leather Belt' (product type). 'Austin' appears to be a model/style name. In the invoice: 'Belt Women Leather' and 'Belt Woman Leather' — contains 'Belt' and 'Leather'. Checking if 'Leather Belt' can be identified in invoice text.

comparison_steps.step: 4.3.3.2 - Certificate main designation as word sequence in invoice
comparison_steps.description: Checking if words from certificate designation appear in invoice
comparison_steps.analysis: 'Leather' appears in invoice ('Belt Women Leather'). 'Belt' appears in invoice. However, the order is reversed: certificate says 'Leather Belt' while invoice says 'Belt ... Leather'. Per rule 4.3.3, matching shall NOT be based on changed word order of the identity-bearing main designation.
comparison_steps.result: Word order differs — but assessing further

comparison_steps.description: Evaluating additions in both documents
comparison_steps.analysis: 'Austin' in certificate is a model/style name — a non-identity-bearing addition (internal reference/model code). 'Women/Woman' in invoice is a product category qualifier. '100% cow leather' in certificate matches '100% COW LEATHER' in invoice (composition). 'Scientific name: Bos Taurus' is supplementary information. 'SPLIT' in invoice is a leather type qualifier. The core product is a leather belt in both documents.

comparison_steps.description: The core product identification
comparison_steps.analysis: Both documents describe a leather belt. Certificate: 'Leather Belt' (with 'Austin' as model name). Invoice: 'Belt Women Leather' / 'Belt Woman Leather'. The word 'Belt' and 'Leather' appear in both. While the exact word order differs ('Leather Belt' vs 'Belt...Leather'), both unambiguously describe the same product category: a leather belt. The composition matches: certificate '100% cow leather' vs invoice '100% COW LEATHER'. The HS code 4203300090 on the invoice corresponds to 'Belts and bandoliers' of leather. Order reference 506470-3509 matches. Only one product type exists in the invoice. Given that 'Belt' and 'Leather' are both present and the composition matches exactly, the product identity is verifiable.

rules_applied.description: Controlled text matching — identity-bearing words 'Belt' and 'Leather' present in both documents with matching composition
Non-identity-bearing additions — 'Austin' (model name), 'Women/Woman' (category), 'Bos Taurus' (scientific name), 'SPLIT' (leather subtype) are non-identity-bearing additions
rules_applied.outcome: Additions do not affect product identity

goods_description.motivation: Certificate describes 'Austin Leather Belt, Composition: 100% cow leather'. Invoice describes 'Belt Women Leather, 100% COW SPLIT LEATHER' and 'Belt Woman Leather, 100% COW LEATHER'. The identity-bearing core words 'Belt' and 'Leather' are present in both documents. The composition '100% cow leather' matches exactly (certificate) vs '100% COW LEATHER' (invoice). 'Austin' is a model/style name (non-identity-bearing per 4.3.4). 'Women/Woman' is a product category qualifier in invoice. 'SPLIT' is a leather processing type qualifier. HS code 4203300090 confirms the product is a leather belt. Same order reference confirms it is the same transaction. Only one product in the invoice. MATCH.
goods_description.debug_explanation: Certificate text: 'Austin Leather Belt, Composition: 100% cow leather. Scientific name: Bos Taurus'. Invoice text: 'Belt Women Leather 100%COW SPLIT LEATHER'. Core product: leather belt in both. Composition: 100% cow leather matches. Model name 'Austin' is non-identity-bearing addition per 4.3.4. Rule 4.3.3 applied for text matching. MATCH.

quantity.invoice_value: Net Weight: 0.90 KG / Gross Weight: 1.25 KG / 1 Cartons

comparison_steps.description: Certificate box 7 contains: 'Net: 0,90' and 'Gross: 1,25'. Box 6 contains '1 carton'. Per rule 4.4.2.1, box 7 is the verification-grounding quantity.
comparison_steps.result: Box 7 values (Net: 0,90 and Gross: 1,25) are verification-grounding

comparison_steps.description: Certificate explicitly states 'Net:' and 'Gross:' — weight categories are explicitly identified (Net Weight and Gross Weight). However, no explicit unit (KG, MT) is stated in certificate.
comparison_steps.result: Weight categories identified. Unit missing in certificate — proceed to 4.4.4

comparison_steps.description: Certificate states Net: 0,90 and Gross: 1,25 without explicit unit. Invoice states Net Weight: 0.90 KG and Gross Weight: 1.25 KG. Per rule 4.4.4, when GW/NW is stated without explicit unit: if invoice confirms same numerical value with a unit, proceed to assess.
comparison_steps.result: Invoice confirms both values with KG unit

comparison_steps.description: Certificate: 0,90 (normalized: 0.90). Invoice: 0.90 KG.
Certificate: 1,25 (normalized: 1.25). Invoice: 1.25 KG.
Certificate box 6: '1 carton'. Invoice: '1 Cartons'.

rules_applied.description: Box 7 is verification-grounding quantity — Net: 0,90 and Gross: 1,25

Weight without explicit unit — certificate states Net and Gross without KG/MT. Invoice confirms both values with KG. No conflicting alternative weight values. Per 4.4.2.2 point 2, single weight values without category confirmed by invoice → MATCH
Numerical value identification — 0.90 and 1.25 both found exactly in invoice
Multiple quantity values — both NW and GW verified as matching

quantity.motivation: Certificate box 7 states Net: 0,90 and Gross: 1,25. Invoice states Net Weight: 0.90 KG and Gross Weight: 1.25 KG. Both numerical values match exactly after decimal notation normalization (comma → period). Certificate does not state explicit weight unit, but invoice confirms both values with KG. Per rule 4.4.2.2, since certificate states both GW and NW values and each can be individually identified in the invoice with matching numerical values under the same weight category labels, verification is satisfied. Additionally, certificate box 6 states '1 carton' which matches invoice '1 Cartons'. All quantity values verified. MATCH.
quantity.debug_explanation: Certificate: 'Net: 0,90 / Gross: 1,25 / 1 carton'. Invoice: 'Net Weight: 0.90 KG / Gross Weight: 1.25 KG / 1 Cartons'. NW: 0.90=0.90 MATCH. GW: 1.25=1.25 MATCH. Cartons: 1=1 MATCH. No unit explicitly stated in certificate but invoice confirms values with KG. Rule 4.4.2.2 applied — both GW and NW values confirmed. MATCH.

country_of_origin.invoice_value: Sweden (Country of Origin of Goods) / Sweden (Country of Manufacture)

comparison_steps.description: Search for origin fields in invoice
found_fields: Country of Origin of Goods: Sweden
comparison_steps.result: Origin explicitly stated in two dedicated fields

comparison_steps.description: Certificate: 'SWEDEN'. Invoice: 'Sweden'.
Only one origin country in both documents: Sweden

rules_applied.description: Origin identified in invoice via 'Country of Origin of Goods: Sweden' and 'Country of Manufacture: Sweden'
Positive verification — Sweden explicitly identified in invoice origin fields

country_of_origin.motivation: Certificate box 3 states origin as 'SWEDEN'. Invoice contains two explicit origin fields: 'Country of Origin of Goods: Sweden' and 'Country of Manufacture: Sweden'. Both confirm Sweden as the origin. After case normalization, SWEDEN = Sweden. Single origin country, no mixed origins. Clear and unambiguous MATCH.
country_of_origin.debug_explanation: Certificate: 'SWEDEN' in box 3. Invoice: 'Country of Origin of Goods: Sweden' and 'Country of Manufacture: Sweden'. Normalization: case only. Rule 4.5.1: origin found in dedicated fields. Rule 4.5.4: SWEDEN=Sweden. MATCH.

evidence.section: Country of Origin of Goods / Country of Manufacture

rule_evaluation_log.result: NOT_APPLICABLE — no LC reference found in certificate

Document A (certificate file) contains certificate on page 1 and embedded invoice copy on page 2. Document B (invoice file) contains the separate invoice. Verification performed against Document B per rule 8.5.0.

VALID — Consignor 'Nordic Leather Group AB' found in invoice Supplier field. Consignee 'H&M Hennes&Mauritz Teksil Ltd. Sti' found in invoice Consignee field. Order reference 506470-3509 matches. All three criteria met.
MATCH — 'Nordic Leather Group AB' found as 'NORDIC LEATHER GROUP AB' in invoice Supplier field

rule_evaluation_log.description: Consignee priority identification — invoice has explicit Consignee field
rule_evaluation_log.result: Invoice Consignee field used as primary verification target
MATCH — 'Teksil' vs 'Tekstil' single character difference, all other name components match
Address differs but per rule 4.2.4 this does not cause MISMATCH when name and country match

rule_evaluation_log.description: Controlled text matching for goods description
rule_evaluation_log.result: MATCH — core product 'leather belt' identifiable in both documents with matching composition
Applied — Net: 0,90 and Gross: 1,25 from box 7

rule_evaluation_log.description: Weight unit assessment — certificate lacks explicit unit, invoice confirms with KG
rule_evaluation_log.result: Both NW and GW values confirmed by invoice with matching categories and KG unit
MATCH — 'Country of Origin of Goods: Sweden' and 'Country of Manufacture: Sweden' found

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Consignee MISMATCH due to 'Teksil' vs 'Tekstil' spelling difference
rejected_hypotheses.reason_rejected: The difference is a single character omission ('t' missing from 'Tekstil'). Per rule 4.1.3.1, this is NOT a case of 'one word being a DIFFERENT word' — it is the SAME Turkish word with a transcription error. All other name components match exactly. The invoice has an explicit Consignee field with the same H&M entity. Per rule 4.2.4, the address difference (different Istanbul locations) does not affect the assessment. Country matches (Turkiet = Turkey). This is clearly the same entity.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Consignee MISMATCH because invoice Buyer is 'H & M Hennes & Mauritz GBC AB' which differs from certificate consignee
rejected_hypotheses.reason_rejected: Invoice has BOTH a Buyer field AND a Consignee field. Per rule 4.2.0.2, the Consignee field takes priority when it exists. The certificate consignee matches the invoice Consignee field, not the Buyer field. The Buyer is a different H&M entity (GBC AB in Sweden) which is the purchasing entity, while the consignee is the receiving entity in Turkey.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Goods description MISMATCH due to word order difference ('Leather Belt' vs 'Belt Women Leather')
rejected_hypotheses.reason_rejected: Both documents describe a leather belt. The identity-bearing words 'Belt' and 'Leather' are present in both. The composition '100% cow leather' matches exactly. 'Austin' is a model name (non-identity-bearing per 4.3.4). 'Women/Woman' is a product category qualifier. Only one product in the invoice. HS code confirms belt product category. The core product identity is verifiable.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Quantity MISMATCH because certificate does not state weight unit (KG/MT)
rejected_hypotheses.reason_rejected: Per rule 4.4.2.2, when certificate states weight values with GW/NW labels but without explicit unit, and invoice confirms the same numerical values with a unit (KG), this is handled by rule 4.4.4. Since both NW and GW values match exactly and no conflicting values exist, verification is satisfied.

overall_assessment.human_explanation: Alla kontrollpunkter överensstämmer. Avsändare Nordic Leather Group AB återfinns som leverantör i fakturan. Mottagare H&M Hennes&Mauritz Teksil Ltd. Sti identifieras i fakturans mottagarfält (med en bokstavsavvikelse: 'Teksil' istället för 'Tekstil' — en uppenbar skrivvariation). Varubeskrivningen avser läderbälten i båda dokumenten med matchande materialsammansättning. Nettovikt 0,90 och bruttovikt 1,25 stämmer exakt. Ursprungsland Sverige bekräftas i fakturans ursprungsfält. Dokumentparet kan godkännas automatiskt.
warnings: Minor spelling variation in consignee name: certificate 'Teksil' vs invoice 'Tekstil' (single character omission — likely transcription error)

human_readable_report.title: Verifieringsrapport – Certificate of Origin mot Faktura

sections.certificate_value: Nordic Leather Group AB, Kärranäsvägen 24, SE-451 76, UDDEVALLA, Sweden
sections.invoice_value: NORDIC LEATHER GROUP AB, KARRANASVAGEN 24, 451 76 UDDEVALLA Vaestra Goetaland C., Sweden, TAX ID: SE5566881693
sections.rule_applied: Direkt identifiering av consignor i fakturans leverantörsfält (Supplier). Företagsnamn identiskt efter versal-/gemenennormalisering. Land Sverige bekräftat via explicit adress och SE-prefix i TAX ID.
sections.motivation: Certifikatets avsändare 'Nordic Leather Group AB' identifieras direkt som fakturans leverantör 'NORDIC LEATHER GROUP AB'. Land Sverige bekräftas. MATCH.

sections.certificate_value: H&M Hennes&Mauritz Teksil Ltd. Sti, Abdi Ipecki Cad o. 19/1 Kat 5, TR-Nisantasi-Sisli-Istanbul, Turkiet
sections.invoice_value: H&M Hennes & Mauritz Tekstil Ltd. Sti. TIN 454 051, 1083-Beyoglu V.D. c/o APL Logistics & Netlog, Akcaburgaz Mah. 1567. Sok. No:2 (Esenyurt 1 Warehouse), Esenyurt Istanbul 34522, Turkey
sections.rule_applied: Mottagare verifierad mot fakturans explicita Consignee-fält. Enstaka teckenavvikelse 'Teksil'/'Tekstil' bedömd som skrivvariation (ett tecken). Adressskillnad påverkar inte bedömningen per regel 4.2.4. Land Turkiet=Turkey.
sections.motivation: Certifikatets mottagare 'H&M Hennes&Mauritz Teksil Ltd. Sti' identifieras i fakturans Consignee-fält som 'H&M Hennes & Mauritz Tekstil Ltd. Sti.' Enda namnsskillnaden är 'Teksil' vs 'Tekstil' (ett 't' saknas — uppenbar skrivvariation). Alla övriga namndelar matchar. Adresser skiljer sig (olika Istanbul-adresser) men detta påverkar inte bedömningen. Land: Turkiet = Turkey. MATCH.

sections.certificate_value:
Austin Leather Belt
Composition: 100% cow leather. Scientific name: Bos Taurus
sections.invoice_value: Belt Women Leather, 100%COW SPLIT LEATHER / Belt Woman Leather, 100% COW LEATHER
sections.rule_applied: Kontrollerad textmatchning. Identitetsbärande kärnord 'Belt' och 'Leather' identifieras i båda dokumenten. Materialsammansättning '100% cow leather' matchar. Modellnamnet 'Austin' och kategoribeteckningen 'Women' är icke-identitetsbärande tillägg.
sections.motivation: Båda dokumenten beskriver ett läderbälte. Certifikat: 'Austin Leather Belt, 100% cow leather'. Faktura: 'Belt Women Leather, 100% COW LEATHER'. Kärnorden 'Belt' och 'Leather' finns i båda. Sammansättningen matchar. MATCH.

sections.invoice_value: Net Weight: 0.90 KG / Gross Weight: 1.25 KG / 1 Cartons
sections.rule_applied: Numerisk verifiering av box 7-värden. Nettovikt 0,90 = 0.90 KG. Bruttovikt 1,25 = 1.25 KG. Försändelsekvantitet 1 carton = 1 Cartons.
sections.motivation: Certifikatets viktvärden (Net: 0,90 och Gross: 1,25) matchar exakt fakturans viktvärden (Net Weight: 0.90 KG och Gross Weight: 1.25 KG). Kolli: 1 carton = 1 Cartons. MATCH.

sections.invoice_value: Sweden (Country of Origin of Goods) / Sweden (Country of Manufacture)
sections.rule_applied: Direkt identifiering av ursprungsland i fakturans dedicerade ursprungsfält. SWEDEN = Sweden efter versalnormalisering.
sections.motivation: Certifikatets ursprungsland 'SWEDEN' bekräftas av fakturans 'Country of Origin of Goods: Sweden' och 'Country of Manufacture: Sweden'. MATCH.

human_readable_report.summary: Samtliga fem kontrollpunkter (avsändare, mottagare, varubeskrivning, kvantitet, ursprungsland) ger MATCH. En mindre skrivvariation noteras i mottagarnamnet ('Teksil' vs 'Tekstil'). Dokumentparet bedöms som IDENTICAL och rekommenderas för automatiskt godkännande.

output_quality_checks.notes: All applicable checkpoints MATCH with confidence ≥ 0.85. Lowest checkpoint confidence is 0.92 (consignee) which is above the 0.85 floor. comparison_result IDENTICAL is consistent with all checkpoints being MATCH.
